# Contributing

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Node.js | >=22 (see `engines.node` in `package.json`) | CI runs on Node 24 |
| npm | Latest | `npm install -g npm@latest` |

## Getting Started

```bash
# Clone and install
git clone git@github.com:contentful/contentful-sdk-core.git
cd contentful-sdk-core
npm ci

# Build
npm run build

# Run tests
npm test
```

## Development Workflow

```bash
# Watch mode for tests (re-runs on file changes)
npm run test:watch
```

## Commands

| Command | What it does | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run build` | Clean dist/ and produce ESM + CJS + types via Rollup | `package.json` → `scripts.build` |
| `npm test` | Run all unit tests (Vitest, single run) | `package.json` → `scripts.test` |
| `npm run test:watch` | Run tests in watch mode | `package.json` → `scripts.test:watch` |
| `npm run test:cover` | Run tests with V8 coverage report | `package.json` → `scripts.test:cover` |
| `npm run lint` | ESLint on `src/` and `test/` | `package.json` → `scripts.lint` |
| `npm run prettier:check` | Check formatting without writing | `package.json` → `scripts.prettier:check` |
| `npm run prettier` | Auto-format all JS/TS files | `package.json` → `scripts.prettier` |
| `npm run clean` | Remove `dist/` and `coverage/` | `package.json` → `scripts.clean` |

> **Note:** There is no standalone `tsc --noEmit` script. Type checking runs
> during `npm run build` via Rollup's TypeScript plugin (`noEmitOnError: true`).
> A failed type check will fail the build.

## Testing

- **Framework:** Vitest
- **Location:** `test/unit/` — files follow `<module-name>-test.spec.ts` or `<module-name>.spec.ts` naming
- **Run all:** `npm test`
- **Run single:** `npx vitest run test/unit/<file>.spec.ts`
- **Watch mode:** `npm run test:watch`
- **Coverage:** `npm run test:cover` (V8 provider via `@vitest/coverage-v8`)

Tests use `axios-mock-adapter` to mock HTTP responses. Vitest is configured with `globals: true` (no explicit imports needed for `describe`/`it`/`expect`).

## Code Style & Conventions

- **Formatting:** Prettier (config in `package.json` → `lint-staged`). Runs automatically on staged files via Husky pre-commit hook.
- **Linting:** ESLint with `eslint:recommended` + `@typescript-eslint` + `prettier`. Config: `.eslintrc.cjs`.
- **TypeScript:** Strict mode, target ESNext, module resolution "bundler". Config: `tsconfig.json`.
- **Imports:** Use `.js` extensions in TypeScript source files (required for ESM resolution).

## Sensitive Files

| Path | Why it needs extra care |
|---|---|
| `src/create-http-client.ts` | Interceptor attachment order is load-bearing; changes affect all downstream SDK API calls |
| `src/rate-limit.ts` | Retry logic for 429/5xx — incorrect changes can cause retry storms or silent failures |
| `src/rate-limit-throttle.ts` | Proactive throttle that auto-calibrates from API headers; breakage causes rate limit violations |
| `src/pThrottle.ts` | Internalized third-party code — changes must preserve the throttle contract |
| `src/error-handler.ts` | Auth token redaction happens here — bugs can leak credentials in logs |
| `rollup.config.mjs` | Produces both ESM and CJS outputs; misconfiguration breaks downstream consumers |

## Adding a New Utility Module

Use `src/freeze-sys.ts` as a minimal pattern: single default export, typed,
with a corresponding test at `test/unit/<name>-test.spec.ts`. Export the new
module from `src/index.ts` if it's part of the public API.

## Commit Convention

This repo uses [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) enforced via Commitizen:

```
type(scope): description
```

Valid types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `refactor`, `test`, `perf`, `ci`, `build`, `revert`

Examples:
```
feat: add support for custom request interceptors
fix(rate-limit): respect x-contentful-ratelimit-reset header
chore(deps): bump axios to 1.13.x
```

A pre-commit hook runs `lint-staged` (Prettier + ESLint) on staged files via Husky.

## Branch Strategy

- `master` — production; all releases cut from here
- `next` — pre-release channel (`npm install contentful-sdk-core@next`)
- `beta` — pre-release channel (`npm install contentful-sdk-core@beta`)
- `dev` — dev pre-release channel
- Feature branches — `feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/` prefixes

## Release Process

Fully automated via `semantic-release` on push to `master`:

1. Commit analyzer determines version bump from conventional commit types
2. Changelog is generated and committed
3. Package is published to npm
4. GitHub release is created

Pre-release branches (`beta`, `dev`) publish to their respective npm dist-tags automatically.

## Pull Requests

- Use conventional commit format for PR title
- CI must pass: build, lint, prettier:check, test:cover
- Merge strategy: merge commit (not squash — semantic-release reads individual commits)

## CI/CD

| Job | Trigger | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `build` | Push/PR to any branch | Installs deps, runs `npm run build`, caches `dist/` |
| `check` | After build passes | Runs linter, prettier check, and tests with coverage |
| `release` | Push to `master`/`beta`/`dev` | Runs `semantic-release` to publish to npm |
| `codeql` | Scheduled + push | GitHub CodeQL security scanning |
| `dependabot-approve` | Dependabot PRs | Auto-approves and requests merge for dependency updates |
| `failure-notification` | CI failure on main branches | Notifies team of pipeline failures |
